error.missingRedirectUrl: Sign-in return URL is not configured.

safety.selfSupportBoundary: Flare is a self-support tool. It does not monitor you in real time or contact anyone for you.
safety.notCrisisCare: Flare is for self-support and reflection. It is not therapy, medical treatment, or a crisis service.
safety.noEmergencyResponse: Flare does not monitor urgent risk or provide emergency response.
safety.urgentHelp: If you may hurt yourself or someone else, or need immediate help, contact local emergency services or a crisis hotline now.

loading.copy: Checking whether your setup can be saved.

authenticated.copy: Your Behavior Pattern and Anchor Note can now reload when you sign in on this device.

clientUnavailable.copy: Your setup is only saved on this device because sign-in is not fully configured.

noSession.copy: Your setup can work locally for now. Sign in to save it and use it across devices.

modes.existingAccountDetail: Sign in with an existing account.
modes.firstTimeSetupDetail: Create an account if this is your first time setting up Flare.

noSession.passwordHint: Magic link sign-in will send a secure sign-in link to your email.
noSession.redirectMagicLinkPrefix: Magic link sent. It should return to
noSession.redirectMagicLinkFallback: Magic link sent. The sign-in link will use the configured return URL.
noSession.signUpNotice: Account created if sign-up is enabled. Check your email if confirmation is required.

customize.title: Customize what helps you pause
customize.subtitle: Set up the words and plan steps you want available during a flare.

aboutFlare.title: About Flare
aboutFlare.copy: Reopen the first-use welcome if you want a quick reminder of what Flare is for, what it helps you hold onto, and what sign-in adds.

welcome.title: Hold on to your support and your clarity
welcome.subtitle: Difficult moments can make it hard to reach your support and your clarity. Flare helps you reconnect with both.
welcome.intro: Prepare them while you are thinking clearly, so they are ready when you need them most. Gather the reminders, plans, and encouragement you want available before a difficult moment, so you do not have to invent them when you are already overwhelmed.

sections.supportCopy: The people and encouragement you have chosen to keep close when you need them.
sections.clarityCopy: The plans, reminders, and intentions you created while thinking clearly.
sections.signedInCopy: You can begin without an account. Signing in adds saved setup across sessions, account-backed history and plan reloads, and account-based features like Support Group.
sections.alreadySignedInCopy: You are already signed in, so your setup can be saved to your account, reloaded when you return, and used with account-based features like Support Group.
sections.clientUnavailableCopy: You can still begin here, but account saving is not available in this build yet, so your setup stays on this device.
sections.nextStepCopy: Get started by preparing the support words, reminders, and next steps you want ready before you need them.

flare.title: Pause, remember your reasons, and choose a next step
flare.subtitle: Use one simple action to open your saved support words and mark the moment.

setupHero.title: Finish setting up Flare
setupHero.copy: Set up your saved support so Flare is ready when you need it.
setupHero.signedOutCopy: You can start setup on this device now. Sign in when you want to save it and use it across devices.
setupHero.clientUnavailableCopy: You can keep setting things up on this device now. Sign-in is not available yet, so your setup stays local for now.

readiness.title: Readiness
readiness.localOnlyUntilConfigLoaded: Not saved until sign-in is configured

secondaryAction.label: Checkpoint / Reflection
secondaryAction.copy: Open a short reflection without leaving this screen.

history.title: Review past flares
history.subtitle: A simple timeline of Flare Events and saved Checkpoint Reflections.
history.intro: Signed-in sessions can reload saved event history. Signed-out sessions stay local-only. Flare does not monitor you in real time or score your behavior.

search.title: No matching Flare Events
search.copy: Try a different word from the behavior snapshot or reflection notes.

archived.title: No archived Flare Events
archived.copy: Archived events stay saved, but are hidden from the default view.

reflected.title: No reflected Flare Events
reflected.copy: Saved Checkpoint Reflections will appear here.

default.title: No Flare Events yet
default.copy: Send Flare to log your first event. Signed-in sessions can reload saved events here.

behaviorPattern.cardCopy: Name the pattern you want help interrupting, along with the cues that usually show up around it.
behaviorPattern.modalSubtitle: Keep it concrete enough to recognize, and simple enough to update.
behaviorPattern.intro: Describe the pattern you want Flare to help you pause and reflect on.
behaviorPattern.summaryEmptyCopy: Add a lightweight pattern so Flare knows what you are trying to pause.

shortDescription.label: Short description
shortDescription.placeholder: What it feels like when the loop starts.

commonTriggers.label: Common triggers
commonTriggers.placeholder: Stress, boredom, being alone, an argument.

riskTimesOrSituations.label: Risk times or situations
riskTimesOrSituations.placeholder: Late nights, payday, after a setback.

flarePlan.cardCopy: Choose a few simple actions you can follow after sending a Flare. You’ll see them one at a time when you need them.
flarePlan.modalSubtitle: Keep it short, direct, and easy to follow in the moment.
flarePlan.helperCopy: These actions are for future Flare moments. Changes here do not alter past activity.
flarePlan.builtInDefaultSummaryCopy: Signed-out Flare still uses the built-in default plan. Sign in to save your own personal plan.
flarePlan.signInRequiredTitle: Sign in to personalize this plan
flarePlan.signInRequiredCopy: Signed-out Flare can still guide you with the built-in default plan. Sign in when you want to save, edit, or sync your own personal plan.
flarePlan.emptyCopy: Add one or two simple steps you can actually follow after sending a Flare.
flarePlan.noTemplatesCopy: No starter actions are available right now.
flarePlan.removeBody: It will no longer appear in your Flare Plan. Past Flare activity will not be changed.

title.label: Title
title.placeholder: Put my phone in another room

description.label: Description (optional)
description.placeholder: Leave it there for at least ten minutes.

anchorNote.cardCopy: Save the reminders, costs, and phrases you want Flare to show when you need a pause.
anchorNote.modalSubtitle: Write grounded words you want available during a flare.
anchorNote.intro: Keep this honest, specific, and easy to revisit. This note is for self-support.
anchorNote.helperCopy: Save requires one reason to pause and one supportive phrase.
anchorNote.summaryEmptyCopy: Save a few grounded reminders so Flare can show them during a flare.

interruptionReasons.label: Why pause this pattern?
interruptionReasons.placeholder: I want to stop before I lose the rest of tonight.

continuingCosts.label: Costs of continuing
continuingCosts.placeholder: I will feel foggy tomorrow and break trust with myself again.

groundedReminders.label: Reminder from grounded self
groundedReminders.placeholder: This urge rises and passes. I do not have to obey it.

emergencyActions.label: Immediate next step
emergencyActions.placeholder: Put the phone in the kitchen, drink water, and walk outside.

supportChannel.cardCopy: Connect one GroupMe group for a single saved Flare support message. Send a test before relying on it.
supportChannel.modalSubtitle: Choose one GroupMe group, confirm the saved message, and test it from here.

currentStatus.title: Current status
currentStatus.copy: This setting is for one calm, predefined support signal. It is not a chat thread.

boundary.title: What this does
boundary.copy: Flare can send one predefined message to the GroupMe group you choose.
boundary.supportersCopy: Your supporters do not need to install Flare. You stay in control of which group is connected.

signInRequired.title: Sign in first
signInRequired.copy: Support group settings are tied to your account, so sign in before connecting GroupMe.

connect.title: 1. Connect GroupMe
connect.copy: Authorize GroupMe so Flare can show your groups and set up one support destination.
connect.reconnectCopy: Reconnect GroupMe if you want to refresh the connection or turn this support group back on.

choose.title: 2. Choose a group
choose.copy: Pick the GroupMe group that should receive your Flare support signal.

message.title: 3. Confirm the saved message
message.copy: This is the message Flare will send when you press Send Flare. It is not editable at send time.

test.title: 4. Send a test
test.copy: Send one test flare to make sure the right group sees it before you depend on it.

lastDelivery.empty: No test or delivery recorded yet

notice.chooseGroup: GroupMe is connected. Choose the group you want Flare to use.
notice.disabled: Support group disabled. Reconnect when you want to use it again.
notice.saved: Support group saved. Send a test before you rely on it.

errors.connectFailed: GroupMe could not be connected right now.
errors.disableFailed: Support group could not be disabled.
errors.loadFailed: Support group settings could not be loaded.
errors.saveFailed: Support group settings could not be saved.
errors.testFailed: Test flare could not be sent.

sendFlare.label: Send Flare
sendFlare.hint: Starts a new Flare Event and opens Flare Response.
sendFlare.copy: Mark this moment, open your saved support words, and choose a next step.

flareResponse.modalSubtitle: Your saved support words are attached to this Flare Event.
flareResponse.defaultWhy: You already created a pause. Stay with the reason you chose recovery.
flareResponse.defaultConsequences: If you have not saved consequences yet, keep this moment simple and move to your next safe step.
flareResponse.safety: If you may hurt yourself or someone else, or need immediate help, contact local emergency services or a crisis hotline now.
flareResponse.defaultNextStep: Step away from the trigger for two minutes, drink water, and give yourself a short pause before choosing what comes next.

externalSupport.label: Support Group Delivery
externalSupport.sendingCopy: Flare is trying the saved support delivery in the background.
externalSupport.sentCopy: Your saved support message was sent to the connected group.
externalSupport.failedCopy: Your flare was still recorded here, but the external support message did not go through.
externalSupport.blockedCopy: Flare kept the event, but the support group could not receive a message right now.
externalSupport.notConfiguredCopy: Set up a support group in Customize if you want Send Flare to also notify someone.
externalSupport.disabledCopy: Reconnect the saved group in Customize before Send Flare can notify it.

checkpointReflection.modalSubtitle: Keep it short and attach it to the current event.
checkpointReflection.helperCopy: Save requires an active event and the main reflection fields.

context.activeCopy: This reflection will be saved to the event you started from Send Flare.
context.missingCopy: Send Flare first to create an event, then save a reflection here.

checkpointReflection.intro: Notice what happened, what helped, and what you want to remember next time.

whatHappened.label: What happened?
whatHappened.placeholder: I hit the urge after getting home and started pacing.

whatHelped.label: What helped?
whatHelped.placeholder: I stepped outside and texted a friend instead.

howIFeelNow.label: How do I feel now?
howIFeelNow.placeholder: Still activated, but less locked in.

outcome.label: Outcome
outcome.supportingCopy: Choose the closest outcome for this event. Add nuance in the optional note below.

note.label: Optional note
note.placeholder: The first two minutes were the hardest.

list.behaviorPatternNotConfigured: Behavior Pattern: Not set up yet

detailModal.title: Flare Event details
detailModal.subtitle: Review the saved behavior snapshot and attached reflection.
